entry.text: It shouldn't be intact. The alley has been picked over, the rubble sorted, everything of value extracted by whoever worked this block before you. But under a collapsed shelf unit, sealed behind a pane of intact glass that must have come down just right and never been disturbed: a bottle. Heavy. Pristine. Filled to the shoulder with a liquid that is an unreasonable shade of orange — not amber, not rust, orange, the colour of something actively deciding to be noticed. It glows, faintly, on its own.

choices.label: Take it carefully — this could be valuable
Open it immediately and take a sip — curiosity wins

bottle_retrieved.text: The glass pane comes away cleanly. The bottle is heavier than it looks and the seal is original — a glass stopper with a wax ring that has survived everything. Whatever this is, it was made to last. The orange light pulses once, faintly, as it settles into your pack.

bottle_sampled.text: A single sip is enough to understand what you're dealing with. Your vision fills instantly with butterflies — vivid, luminescent, erupting from the edges of your sight in cascading impossible colours. You are, for about twenty seconds, the most well-rested and also most confused person in the wasteland. Then it passes. You pocket the rest of the bottle very carefully.

glass_shelf_collapse.text: The shelf unit chose this moment to finish falling. The glass pane shatters and you pull back fast but catch a long shallow cut across your palm from the leading edge.
